creator: freebsd-2024@virtual-estates.net
text:
(In reply to Marek Zarychta from comment #9)
> That's not how it works.

That's exactly, how it works, Marek, in any organization: people, who make a mistake, get an earful -- which reduces their chance of reoffending.

And it was, objectively, a mistake -- the port of ownCloud was broken by this action, unbeknown to those, who removed php-7.4. Had it been deliberate, the port would've been disabled (or even removed) too.

> The reason for dropping support for PHP 7.4 is clear to me

Drop of upstream support may be a good reason to "sunset" a port. Maybe. But it is not a reason to delete it altogether so quickly. It is perfectly obvious, no one even realized, ownCloud will be affected.

I don't blame anyone for the non-realizing -- the port is still giving a mistaken impression of being usable with any PHP version instead of insisting on 7.4. But I do blame the haste -- had the sunset period been 6-12 months, the chances of someone noticing the upcoming problem would've been much better.

> Please feel free to use already installed PHP 7.4, migrate to Nextcloud, migrate to Linux or do what really suits your needs.

Frankly, I'd rather the trigger-happy busy bodies "migrated to Linux" instead.
